steps.title: Identify how margin of error depends on sample size
steps.content: The margin of error formula is: $MOE = z^* \sqrt{\frac{p(1-p)}{n}}$. Since $n$ appears in the denominator, increasing sample size decreases margin of error. This is an inverse relationship: a larger sample produces a smaller margin of error.

steps.title: Calculate the effect of increasing sample size
steps.content:
Original sample: $n = 144$, MOE = 5.64%. New sample: $n = 288$ (doubled). When $n$ doubles, $\sqrt{n}$ increases by a factor of $\sqrt{2} \approx 1.41$.

Therefore, the new MOE is approximately $\frac{5.64\%}{1.41} \approx 4.00\%$ — smaller than before.

The estimate itself (around 76%) stays roughly the same. A is correct.
